29	func TestTaskBucket_Update(t *testing.T) {
30		u := &user.User{ID: 1}
31	
32		t.Run("full bucket", func(t *testing.T) {
33			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
34			s := db.NewSession()
35			defer s.Close()
36	
37			tb := &TaskBucket{
38				TaskID:        1,
39				BucketID:      2, // Bucket 2 already has 3 tasks and a limit of 3
40				ProjectViewID: 4,
41				ProjectID:     1, // In actual web requests set via the url
42			}
43	
44			err := tb.Update(s, u)
45			require.Error(t, err)
46			assert.True(t, IsErrBucketLimitExceeded(err))
47		})
48		t.Run("full bucket but not changing the bucket", func(t *testing.T) {
49			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
50			s := db.NewSession()
51			defer s.Close()
52	
53			tb := &TaskBucket{
54				TaskID:        4,
55				BucketID:      2, // Bucket 2 already has 3 tasks and a limit of 3
56				ProjectViewID: 4,
57				ProjectID:     1, // In actual web requests set via the url
58			}
59			err := tb.Update(s, u)
60			require.NoError(t, err)
61		})
62		t.Run("bucket on other project view", func(t *testing.T) {
63			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
64			s := db.NewSession()
65			defer s.Close()
66	
67			tb := &TaskBucket{
68				TaskID:        1,
69				BucketID:      4, // Bucket 4 belongs to project 2
70				ProjectViewID: 4,
71				ProjectID:     1, // In actual web requests set via the url
72			}
73			err := tb.Update(s, u)
74			require.Error(t, err)
75			assert.True(t, IsErrBucketDoesNotBelongToProject(err))
76		})
77		t.Run("moving a task to the done bucket", func(t *testing.T) {
78			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
79			s := db.NewSession()
80			defer s.Close()
81	
82			tb := &TaskBucket{
83				TaskID:        1,
84				BucketID:      3, // Bucket 3 is the done bucket
85				ProjectViewID: 4,
86				ProjectID:     1, // In actual web requests set via the url
87			}
88			err := tb.Update(s, u)
89			require.NoError(t, err)
90			err = s.Commit()
91			require.NoError(t, err)
92			assert.True(t, tb.Task.Done)
93	
94			db.AssertExists(t, "tasks", map[string]interface{}{
95				"id":   1,
96				"done": true,
97			}, false)
98			db.AssertExists(t, "task_buckets", map[string]interface{}{
99				"task_id":   1,
100				"bucket_id": 3,
101			}, false)
102		})
103		t.Run("move done task out of done bucket", func(t *testing.T) {
104			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
105			s := db.NewSession()
106			defer s.Close()
107	
108			tb := &TaskBucket{
109				TaskID:        2,
110				BucketID:      1, // Bucket 1 is the default bucket
111				ProjectViewID: 4,
112				ProjectID:     1, // In actual web requests set via the url
113			}
114			err := tb.Update(s, u)
115			require.NoError(t, err)
116			err = s.Commit()
117			require.NoError(t, err)
118			assert.False(t, tb.Task.Done)
119	
120			db.AssertExists(t, "tasks", map[string]interface{}{
121				"id":   tb.TaskID,
122				"done": false,
123			}, false)
124			db.AssertExists(t, "task_buckets", map[string]interface{}{
125				"task_id":   tb.TaskID,
126				"bucket_id": 1,
127			}, false)
128			db.AssertMissing(t, "task_buckets", map[string]interface{}{
129				"task_id":   tb.TaskID,
130				"bucket_id": 3,
131			})
132		})
133		t.Run("moving a repeating task to the done bucket", func(t *testing.T) {
134			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
135			s := db.NewSession()
136			defer s.Close()
137	
138			tb := &TaskBucket{
139				TaskID:        28,
140				BucketID:      3, // Bucket 3 is the done bucket
141				ProjectViewID: 4,
142				ProjectID:     1, // In actual web requests set via the url
143			}
144	
145			// Before running the TaskBucket Update we retrieve the task and execute
146			// an updateDone to obtain the task with updated start/end/due dates
147			// This way we can later match them with what happens after running TaskBucket Update
148			u := &user.User{ID: 1}
149			oldTask := &Task{ID: tb.TaskID}
150			err := oldTask.ReadOne(s, u)
151			require.NoError(t, err)
152			updatedTask := &Task{ID: tb.TaskID}
153			err = updatedTask.ReadOne(s, u)
154			require.NoError(t, err)
155			updatedTask.Done = true
156			updateDone(oldTask, updatedTask) // updatedTask now contains the updated dates
157	
158			err = tb.Update(s, u)
159			require.NoError(t, err)
160			err = s.Commit()
161			require.NoError(t, err)
162			assert.False(t, tb.Task.Done)
163			assert.Equal(t, int64(1), tb.BucketID) // This should be the actual bucket
164	
165			db.AssertExists(t, "tasks", map[string]interface{}{
166				"id":   1,
167				"done": false,
168			}, false)
169			db.AssertExists(t, "task_buckets", map[string]interface{}{
170				"task_id":   1,
171				"bucket_id": 1,
172			}, false)
173	
174			assert.Equal(t, updatedTask.DueDate.Unix(), tb.Task.DueDate.Unix())
175			assert.Equal(t, updatedTask.StartDate.Unix(), tb.Task.StartDate.Unix())
176			assert.Equal(t, updatedTask.EndDate.Unix(), tb.Task.EndDate.Unix())
177		})
178	
179		t.Run("keep done timestamp when moving task between projects", func(t *testing.T) {
180			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
181			u := &user.User{ID: 1}
182	
183			doneAt := time.Now().Round(time.Second)
184	
185			// Set a done timestamp on the task
186			func() {
187				s := db.NewSession()
188				defer s.Close()
189				_, err := s.ID(2).Cols("done_at").Update(&Task{DoneAt: doneAt})
190				require.NoError(t, err)
191				err = s.Commit()
192				require.NoError(t, err)
193			}()
194	
195			// Move the task to another project without a done bucket using Task.Update
196			func() {
197				s := db.NewSession()
198				defer s.Close()
199				task := &Task{ID: 2, Done: true, ProjectID: 9}
200				err := task.Update(s, u)
201				require.NoError(t, err)
202				err = s.Commit()
203				require.NoError(t, err)
204			}()
205	
206			// Verify the task still has the same done timestamp
207			func() {
208				s := db.NewSession()
209				defer s.Close()
210				var task Task
211				_, err := s.ID(2).Get(&task)
212				require.NoError(t, err)
213				assert.True(t, task.Done)
214				assert.WithinDuration(t, doneAt, task.DoneAt, time.Second)
215			}()
216	
217			// Move the task back to the original project with a done bucket using Task.Update
218			func() {
219				s := db.NewSession()
220				defer s.Close()
221				task := &Task{ID: 2, Done: true, ProjectID: 1}
222				err := task.Update(s, u)
223				require.NoError(t, err)
224				err = s.Commit()
225				require.NoError(t, err)
226			}()
227	
228			// Verify the done timestamp is still preserved
229			func() {
230				s := db.NewSession()
231				defer s.Close()
232				var task Task
233				_, err := s.ID(2).Get(&task)
234				require.NoError(t, err)
235				assert.True(t, task.Done)
236				assert.WithinDuration(t, doneAt, task.DoneAt, time.Second)
237			}()
238		})
239	}

32	func TestTask_Create(t *testing.T) {
33		usr := &user.User{
34			ID:       1,
35			Username: "user1",
36			Email:    "user1@example.com",
37		}
38	
39		// We only test creating a task here, the permissions are all well tested in the web tests.
40	
41		t.Run("normal", func(t *testing.T) {
42			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
43			s := db.NewSession()
44			defer s.Close()
45	
46			task := &Task{
47				Title:       "Lorem",
48				Description: "Lorem Ipsum Dolor",
49				ProjectID:   1,
50			}
51			err := task.Create(s, usr)
52			require.NoError(t, err)
53			// Assert getting a uid
54			assert.NotEmpty(t, task.UID)
55			// Assert getting a new index
56			assert.NotEmpty(t, task.Index)
57			assert.Equal(t, int64(34), task.Index)
58			err = s.Commit()
59			require.NoError(t, err)
60	
61			db.AssertExists(t, "tasks", map[string]interface{}{
62				"id":            task.ID,
63				"title":         "Lorem",
64				"description":   "Lorem Ipsum Dolor",
65				"project_id":    1,
66				"created_by_id": 1,
67			}, false)
68			db.AssertExists(t, "task_buckets", map[string]interface{}{
69				"task_id":   task.ID,
70				"bucket_id": 1,
71			}, false)
72	
73			events.DispatchPending(s)
74			events.AssertDispatched(t, &TaskCreatedEvent{})
75		})
76		t.Run("with reminders", func(t *testing.T) {
77			db.LoadAndAssertFixtures(t)
78			s := db.NewSession()
79			defer s.Close()
80	
81			task := &Task{
82				Title:       "Lorem",
83				Description: "Lorem Ipsum Dolor",
84				ProjectID:   1,
85				DueDate:     time.Date(2023, time.March, 7, 22, 5, 0, 0, time.UTC),
86				StartDate:   time.Date(2023, time.March, 7, 22, 5, 10, 0, time.UTC),
87				EndDate:     time.Date(2023, time.March, 7, 22, 5, 20, 0, time.UTC),
88				Reminders: []*TaskReminder{
89					{
90						RelativeTo:     "due_date",
91						RelativePeriod: 1,
92					},
93					{
94						RelativeTo:     "start_date",
95						RelativePeriod: -2,
96					},
97					{
98						RelativeTo:     "end_date",
99						RelativePeriod: -1,
100					},
101					{
102						Reminder: time.Date(2023, time.March, 7, 23, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
103					},
104				}}
105			err := task.Create(s, usr)

Implemented the fix without committing.

What changed:
- Updated `pkg/models/kanban_task_bucket.go`.
- When a repeating task is moved into the done bucket, it now:
  - applies the recurrence update,
  - resets the task to not done,
  - moves the task to the kanban view’s default bucket instead of leaving it in its previous bucket.
- Removed the old `updateBucket` skip path so the recalculated default bucket is persisted.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test that reproduced the bug:
  - moved recurring task `28` to a non-default bucket,
  - then moved it to the done bucket,
  - verified it should end up in bucket `1` (To-Do/default).
- Confirmed the temporary test failed before the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary test passed after the fix:
  - `go run github.com/magefile/mage test:filter TestTaskBucket_Update/moving_a_repeating_task_to_the_done_bucket`
- Removed the temporary test changes with `git restore`.

Final working tree:
- Only `pkg/models/kanban_task_bucket.go` is modified.
- There is also an untracked existing `reports/` directory, left untouched.
